FULL CONTENT:
# From Particle Physics to Innovation: Detector Technologies

Université de Genève (UNIGE)
3 likes
87 views
28 Apr 2025
How can cutting-edge particle physics contribute to technological innovation beyond fundamental research?

In this video, Domenico Della Volpe, professor at the Department of Nuclear and Particle Physics (Faculty of Science, University of Geneva), presents the development of advanced particle detectors that not only serve major scientific experiments but also inspire applications in other sectors.

From medical imaging to security technologies, the innovations born in particle physics have the potential to impact society far beyond the laboratory.

🎥 This capsule was filmed as part of the Faculty of Science’s participation at the 50th International Exhibition of Inventions Geneva (April 2025), highlighting the bridges between fundamental science and real-world applications.

📍 Learn more about UNIGE’s involvement at the Salon des Inventions: https://www.unige.ch/lejournal/evenements/a-la-une/lunige-au-salon-des-inventions/

#UNIGE #FacultyofScience #GenevaInventions2025 #ParticlePhysics #Innovation #DetectorTechnology #ResearchToImpact #ScientificDiscovery #ScienceAndSociety

enriched_points.expanded_content: Recent innovations in particle detectors leverage advanced materials and techniques, enhancing sensitivity and precision for high-energy particle detection across various scientific applications.
Emerging technologies enable instantaneous data processing, allowing physicists to analyze particle interactions in real-time, significantly improving experimental efficiency and decision-making.

Advancements in tracking technologies provide improved resolution and accuracy for monitoring particle trajectories, critical for understanding fundamental physics phenomena in high-energy experiments.
